Mandela Day • Book Drive

Give a Book,
Open a World

“Motho ke Motho ka Batho”

In South Africa, 8 out of 10 children still cannot read for meaning by age 10. This Mandela Day, the HM Pitje Foundation is changing that story — putting books into the hands of learners across our network of adopted schools and building a culture of reading that lasts far beyond 67 minutes.

Help a child read Make a contribution
8 in 10SA children can't read for meaning by age 10
2×Higher literacy odds when there are books at home
67 minAll it takes to help start a lifelong reader

Sources: PIRLS 2021 (South Africa) • UNICEF, Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys

Why it matters

A book can change a life

Around the world, literacy is recognised as a fundamental human right and one of the strongest drivers of human development — improving health, reducing poverty and opening doors that stay open for life. Reading builds the cognitive, language and emotional skills that help children think, imagine and grow in empathy. Yet a child's chances often come down to something simple: whether there are books within reach.

For more than a decade, the HM Pitje Foundation has carried the spirit of Nelson Mandela International Day into the classroom. We collect books — new and gently used — and place them directly in the schools we've adopted, helping learners discover the joy of reading and the confidence it builds. Partners join us on the ground: painting bookshelves, running reading circles, and handing books to the children who will treasure them.

Get involved

Three ways to grow a reader

Whatever you can give — books, funds or time — goes straight into the hands of a child who needs it. Every contribution turns a blank shelf into a doorway.

1

Donate books

New or gently used, every book finds a reader. Email us and we'll arrange free collection or delivery — you don't have to lift a thing.

2

Contribute funds

Rather give towards new books? Your contribution helps us buy the right titles for the right ages — and earns a Section 18A tax certificate.

3

Partner with us

Bring your team to a Mandela Day activation — painting libraries, running reading circles, reading alongside learners. Reach out and we'll plan it together.

On the ground

Another way to give

Sponsor a wellness pack

Beyond books, the foundation supports the teachers and learners who keep our adopted schools running. For R67, you can sponsor a wellness pack — one bottle of 60 tablets, a two-month supply.

  • Zinc+ for everyday immune system support
  • Vit-C+ with rose hips
  • Vit-D3 with zinc

R67 = 1 bottle • 60 tablets • 60 days of support

Make a contribution

Banking details

To make a financial contribution, pay into the account below and use your company name + bookdrive as the reference so we can thank you properly.

Want to donate physical books instead? Email hmpfoundation@newgx.co.za to arrange delivery or collection.

As a registered Public Benefit Organisation, every contribution qualifies for a Section 18A tax certificate — so your support gives back to your business too.

Turn a page, change a future

Every book donated and every rand given puts reading within a child's reach — and the research is clear: that's where futures begin. Stand with us this Mandela Day.
